taxonomy: Cladobium epiphytum belongs to the family Cladoniaceae, which is a group of lichens that are commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. This classification places Cladobium epiphytum within the larger group of non-vascular, thalloid lichens. The family Cladoniaceae is further classified under the order Peltigerales and the class Lecanoromycetes. Cladobium epiphytum is a type of crustose lichen, characterized by its flat, crust-like growth habit. The classification of Cladobium epiphytum reflects its unique characteristics and ecological adaptations.
